mechanism_narrative: A higher 3-month growth rate in retail sales indicates resilient household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed companies, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value was 351561 for 2005-10-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce a positive 20-day return spread versus lower-growth observations in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses into a restrictive zone after a rapid tightening, equity discount rates rise faster than expected earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% after rising at least 100bp over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 3.78 for 2005-10-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in unemployment signals weakening labor income and recession risk, causing investors to mark down earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value was 5.0 for 2005-10-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by nonnegative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the trigger return spread versus baseline is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with contained consumer inflation signals real output expansion without margin-eroding price pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is below its 12-month median
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value was 107.055 and CPIAUCSL vintage value was 198.5 for 2005-09-01
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition fails to produce higher 20-day returns than either INDPRO growth or low CPI inflation alone, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp housing starts decline during a rising-rate cycle signals that monetary tightening is impairing credit-sensitive demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% year over year while FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value was 2108.0 for 2005-09-01 and FEDFUNDS vintage value was 3.78 for 2005-10-01
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing-rate stress episodes do not have lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than untriggered observations, or if hit-rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices accelerate while the policy rate is below recent inflation, investors price a larger future tightening response and lower equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 100bp
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value was 198.5 for 2005-09-01 and FEDFUNDS vintage value was 3.78 for 2005-10-01
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS gap trigger has positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth with stable unemployment signals expanding labor income without immediate labor-market deterioration, supporting consumption and earnings, and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 150 thousand over 1 month and UNRATE does not rise month over month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value was 134061.0 and UNRATE vintage value was 5.0 for 2005-10-01
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-unemployment trigger does not outperform the unconditional 5-day return distribution, or if the positive-return hit rate is below the sample base rate.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth indicates broad demand strength and positive aggregate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value was 11193.2 for 2005-07-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if higher GDPC1 growth ranks do not correspond to higher 60-day S&P 500 forward returns in walk-forward tests, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is negative.
